name: evtx
summary: This repo that contains all the Maps used by Eric Zimmerman's EvtxECmd.

documentation.readme: This repo that contains all the Maps used by Eric Zimmerman's EvtxECmd.
usage_sections: EvtxECmd Maps Ideas - Development roadmap for EvtxECmd Maps. Please feel free to contribute by adding ideas or by finishing tasks in the To Do column. Any help is appreciated!

arguments.name: --csv
arguments.description: CLI flag '--csv' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --csvf
arguments.description: CLI flag '--csvf' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --exc
arguments.description: CLI flag '--exc' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: -dt
arguments.description: CLI flag '-dt' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_penetration_testing_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Penetration Testing category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_penetration_testing_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Penetration Testing system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for evtx (EricZimmerman/evtx). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for evtx. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with EricZimmerman/bstrings (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with EricZimmerman/Registry (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with EricZimmerman/MFTECmd (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: This repo that contains all the Maps used by Eric Zimmerman's EvtxECmd.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: This repo that contains all the Maps used by Eric Zimmerman's EvtxECmd.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `evtx` from its upstream repository, then run `evtx --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. This repo that contains all the Maps used by Eric Zimmerman's EvtxECmd.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
